explanationZhTw: 「camp bed」指可折疊、便於攜帶的簡易床，中文可譯為「行軍床／摺疊床」。
generatedExample: I slept on a camp bed while visiting my cousin.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「fixed down」不是一般的「修理」意思，而是指被固定住、綁住或鎖住，無法移動；屬於語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The patient was fixed down so he could not fall off the bed.

explanationZhTw: 「intently」表示「專注地、目不轉睛地」，常用來描述非常認真地看、聽或思考。
generatedExample: She listened intently to every word of the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 「hypodermic syringe」是「皮下注射器」，指醫療上用來注射藥液的針筒。
generatedExample: The nurse prepared a hypodermic syringe before the injection.

explanationZhTw: `take in` 在這裡不是「帶進去」，而是「逐漸理解／看清楚」周遭情況。
generatedExample: It took me a minute to take in the news.

explanationZhTw: `surroundings` 指「周圍環境、四周情況」。
generatedExample: The hotel has beautiful surroundings.

explanationZhTw: `gradually` 表示「逐漸地、慢慢地」。
generatedExample: The room slowly got brighter gradually.

explanationZhTw: `impression` 在這裡是「感覺、印象」，不是正式證據，而是主觀感受。
generatedExample: I had the impression that she was hiding something.

explanationZhTw: `swimming up` 這裡是比喻說法，表示像從深處慢慢浮上來，帶有迷糊、遲鈍地恢復意識的感覺。
generatedExample: After waking, I felt like I was swimming up from a dream.

explanationZhTw: `underwater` 字面是「水下的」，這裡用來形容一個像沉在水底、陌生而壓迫的世界。
generatedExample: They discovered an underwater cave near the coast.

explanationZhTw: `beneath` 表示「在……下方」，也可帶有「低於、在下面」的空間感。
generatedExample: The box was hidden beneath the bed.

explanationZhTw: `arrested` 是「逮捕、拘留」的意思。
generatedExample: Police arrested him late last night.

explanationZhTw: `continuous` 在這裡表示「連續的、不中斷的」。
generatedExample: The rain was continuous all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: `consciousness` 指「意識、知覺狀態」；這裡是比較抽象的心理／清醒狀態。
generatedExample: She slowly regained consciousness after the accident.

explanationZhTw: `consciousness` 指「意識、知覺狀態」；這裡是比較抽象的心理／清醒狀態。
generatedExample: She slowly regained consciousness after the accident.

explanationZhTw: `blank interval` 直譯是「空白的間隔」，這裡指一段記憶或意識中斷、空掉的時間。
generatedExample: There was a blank interval in my memory after the crash.

explanationZhTw: 「preliminary」指「初步的、前置的」。這裡表示那一輪審問只是正式折磨前的開場步驟。
generatedExample: This was only a preliminary check before the real interview.

explanationZhTw: 「interrogation」是「審問、盤問」，通常指帶有壓迫感、由權力方主導的問話。
generatedExample: The police continued the interrogation for several hours.

explanationZhTw: 「were subjected」在這裡表示「被迫接受、遭受」。不是一般的「主動接受」，而是被動地承受某種處置。
generatedExample: The villagers were subjected to strict rules during the crisis.

explanationZhTw: 「as a matter of course」表示「理所當然地、照例地」，強調這件事被當成常規。
generatedExample: As a matter of course, visitors must show ID at the gate.

explanationZhTw: 「simultaneously」是「同時地」。這裡強調多人一起對他施暴。
generatedExample: The two alarms sounded simultaneously.

explanationZhTw: 「went on and on」是慣用說法，表示某事「拖個沒完、一直持續」。
generatedExample: The lecture went on and on, and everyone got restless.

explanationZhTw: 「forsook him」是較文學化的用法，意思是「離他而去、讓他失去支撐」。這裡指他的膽量突然崩潰。
generatedExample: Courage forsook him at the last moment.

explanationZhTw: 「hold out」是片語動詞，意思是「撐住、堅持下去」。這裡指他想忍到痛到不能再忍才開口。
generatedExample: She held out until the meeting finally ended.

explanationZhTw: 「flung like a sack of potatoes」是慣用比喻，表示被粗暴地丟下，毫不在乎對方的感受。
generatedExample: The boxes were flung like a sack of potatoes into the back of the truck.

explanationZhTw: 「recuperate」意思是「恢復體力、康復」。這裡指他被短暫放著休息以便之後再受刑。
generatedExample: He stayed in bed for a day to recuperate after the long trip.

explanationZhTw: 「dimly」在這裡不是「昏暗地」，而是「模糊地、隱約地」。表示記憶不清楚。
generatedExample: I remember the accident only dimly.

explanationZhTw: 「stupor」是「昏沉、麻木狀態」，常指因疲憊、受傷或藥物而意識不清。
generatedExample: After the surgery, he drifted in and out of a stupor.

explanationZhTw: 「surly」指「態度粗暴的、沒好氣的」。這裡形容那位理髮師冷淡又不好惹。
generatedExample: The cashier gave me a surly answer and looked away.

explanationZhTw: 「businesslike」表示「公事公辦的、效率導向的」，帶有冷淡、沒有同情心的感覺。
generatedExample: She gave a businesslike nod and moved straight to the next item.

explanationZhTw: 「unsympathetic」是「不同情的、冷酷的」，這裡強調那些人只顧檢查，不顧他的痛苦。
generatedExample: The manager was unsympathetic to the team's complaints.

explanationZhTw: 「reflexes」是「反射動作、反射神經」。醫護人員會敲打它來檢查神經狀況。
generatedExample: The doctor tested her reflexes with a small hammer.

explanationZhTw: 「at a stretch」表示「一口氣、連續不間斷地」；這裡指時間可連續拉得很長。
generatedExample: She studied for five hours at a stretch before taking a break.

explanationZhTw: 「see to it that...」表示「確保……發生／把……安排妥當」。
generatedExample: Please see to it that the report is sent before noon.

explanationZhTw: 「deliver someone over」表示「把某人交出去、移交給當局或他人處置」。
generatedExample: The suspect was delivered over to the police after the hearing.

explanationZhTw: 「change their tune」是慣用語，表示「改變口風、態度突然轉變」。
generatedExample: Once she showed the receipts, the critics changed their tune.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「破布」，而是比喻神經被折磨得極度脆弱、幾乎崩潰。
generatedExample: After three sleepless nights, my nerves were in rags.

explanationZhTw: 「break someone down」表示「把人逼垮、使其意志或精神崩潰」。
generatedExample: The nonstop pressure finally broke him down.

explanationZhTw: 「sole concern」指「唯一關心的事／唯一在意的焦點」。
generatedExample: Her sole concern was keeping the children safe.

explanationZhTw: 「seditious」指「煽動叛亂的、反政府的」，常用於政治語境。
generatedExample: The government banned the seditious leaflet immediately.

explanationZhTw: 「embezzlement」是法律用語，指「侵占公款、挪用公款」。
generatedExample: She was dismissed after the embezzlement was discovered.

explanationZhTw: 「in the pay of」表示「受雇於……」，常帶有替某方秘密效力的意味。
generatedExample: The columnist was accused of being in the pay of a corporation.

explanationZhTw: 「in the eyes of...」表示「在……看來／依……的觀點」。
generatedExample: In the eyes of her classmates, she was the most reliable person in the room.

explanationZhTw: 表示「另一種／不同類型的」，這裡不是指數量上的另一個，而是指性質不同的一類。
generatedExample: I have memories of another kind from my childhood, mostly sounds and smells.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「特別醒目、特別突出」。這裡指那些記憶在腦中格外明顯地浮現。
generatedExample: Her red coat stood out in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 這個副詞在這裡表示「零散地、彼此沒有連貫地」，描述記憶不是連成一串，而是碎片化出現。
generatedExample: He remembered the trip disconnectedly, with only a few clear scenes.

explanationZhTw: 「cell」在這裡是「牢房、囚室」的意思，指一個很小、封閉的房間。
generatedExample: The prisoner was kept in a cell with no window.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「就在附近、在旁邊」，不是字面上的「在手邊」。
generatedExample: A taxi stand was near at hand, so we left quickly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「instrument」不是「樂器」，而是某種機器或裝置，屬於依上下文判斷的用法。
generatedExample: The room contained some kind of instrument that measured the air pressure.

explanationZhTw: 「ticking」指發出規律的滴答聲，常用來形容鐘錶或類似機器的聲音。
generatedExample: I could hear the clock ticking in the quiet room.

explanationZhTw: 「luminous」表示「發光的、明亮的」。這裡形容眼睛看起來越來越亮。
generatedExample: The luminous sign could be seen from far away.

explanationZhTw: 「swallowed up」表示「被吞沒、被完全包住」，在這裡是帶有強烈畫面感的用法。
generatedExample: The small boat was swallowed up by the fog.

explanationZhTw: `strapped into` 表示「用帶子固定在……裡面／上面」，這裡是把人綁在椅子上，語氣帶有被強制控制的意味。
generatedExample: The pilot was strapped into his seat before takeoff.

explanationZhTw: `dials` 指儀表盤上的圓形刻度盤或旋鈕，屬於機械／設備語境中的詞。
generatedExample: She checked the dials on the old radio until the signal improved.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `tramp` 不是「流浪漢」，而是指沉重腳步聲或踐踏聲，是依上下文才看得出的意思。
generatedExample: We heard the tramp of soldiers marching down the street.

explanationZhTw: `clanged open` 描寫門打開時發出響亮金屬撞擊聲，屬於聲音導向的表達，重點在動作伴隨的聲響。
generatedExample: The gate clanged open and the crowd moved inside.

explanationZhTw: `waxed-faced` 用來形容臉色像蠟一樣，暗示蒼白、僵硬或沒有表情，屬於非常具畫面感的描寫。
generatedExample: The waxed-faced manager listened without showing any reaction.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》的語境裡，`Room 101` 不是一般的「101號房間」，而是象徵極端恐懼與折磨的特殊房間。
generatedExample: He was terrified when they mentioned Room 101.

explanationZhTw: `officer` 指「軍官、警官或執法人員」。這裡是指負責看守或帶領犯人的人。
generatedExample: The officer checked my ID at the gate.

explanationZhTw: 「turn round」在這裡是「轉身、回頭」的意思；屬於日常常用的片語動詞。
generatedExample: She turned round when she heard her name.

explanationZhTw: 「look at」在這裡是「看向、注視」；這是基本常用片語動詞。
generatedExample: Please look at the screen and read the message.

explanationZhTw: 「dials」在這裡不是一般的「撥號盤」概念，而是指儀器上的刻度盤或表盤；屬於依上下文才看得懂的名詞用法。
generatedExample: The engineer checked the dials before starting the machine.

explanationZhTw: 指「用盡全力大聲地、扯開嗓門地」；這裡強調他大聲喊出告解，語氣很強烈。
generatedExample: She shouted his name at the top of her voice across the busy platform.

explanationZhTw: 指「忍住、抑制、沒有說出來」；這裡是說他先前在酷刑下仍努力不吐露的內容。
generatedExample: He tried holding back his anger during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 embedded 不是一般的「嵌入」實體物件，而是比喻「深藏、潛伏在某個時序或可能性裡」；意思偏文學化。
generatedExample: The fear of failure was embedded in the way he planned every step.

explanationZhTw: 指「完全攤開、徹底揭露」；這裡表示他生命中的最後細節已被毫無保留地看見。
generatedExample: The investigation laid bare the company's internal problems.

explanationZhTw: `start up` 在這裡表示「起身、從躺著的狀態開始動作」，不是「啟動機器」。
generatedExample: I was starting up from the sofa when the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: `interrogation` 指「訊問、審問」，常用於警方、軍方或正式調查場合。
generatedExample: The interrogation lasted for three hours.

explanationZhTw: `at his elbow` 是慣用語，表示某人「就在身邊、近在眼前」，帶有幾乎貼身跟隨的感覺。
generatedExample: My coach was at my elbow the whole time I practiced.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「把警衛放到某處」，而是「指使警衛去對付溫斯頓、命令他們採取行動」。
generatedExample: The manager set the security guards on to the intruder.

explanationZhTw: `respite` 指「暫停、喘息的時間、暫時的緩解」，常見於痛苦、壓力或艱難情況中。
generatedExample: The rain gave us a short respite before the storm returned.

explanationZhTw: `pump something into someone` 在這裡表示「把某物強行注入某人體內」，帶有醫療或暴力控制的語氣。
generatedExample: The nurse pumped the medicine into the patient’s vein.

explanationZhTw: `tormentor` 指「折磨別人的人」，比一般的 `enemy` 更強，強調持續施加痛苦。
generatedExample: The bully became the tormentor of the younger students.

explanationZhTw: `inquisitor` 指「審問者、嚴厲盤問的人」；在宗教或歷史語境中也可指「異端裁判官」。
generatedExample: She felt like an inquisitor asking impossible questions.

explanationZhTw: `in my keeping` 是較文學化的說法，表示「由我照看、由我掌管保護」。
generatedExample: The documents are in my keeping until the meeting ends.

explanationZhTw: `watch over` 表示「照看、守護、監護」，比單純的 `watch` 更有保護意味。
generatedExample: My older sister watched over me when I was sick.

explanationZhTw: `turning-point` 指「轉捩點、關鍵時刻」，表示局勢或人生開始明顯改變的時刻。
generatedExample: Passing that exam was a turning-point in her career.

explanationZhTw: 「interrogation」是正式的「訊問、審問」，通常指警方或當局為了取得資訊而進行的盤問，不是一般聊天式的提問。
generatedExample: The detective's interrogation lasted for three hours.

explanationZhTw: 「materialized」在這裡不是「物質化」，而是「逐漸出現、變得清楚可見」；用來描述他對周圍環境的感知慢慢恢復。
generatedExample: After the fog lifted, the buildings materialized around us.

explanationZhTw: 「flat on his back」是慣用說法，表示「平躺著、仰躺著」；也可暗示人無法動彈或病得很重。
generatedExample: I slipped on the ice and ended up flat on my back.

explanationZhTw: 「held down」在這裡是被動式的片語動詞，意思是「被壓住、被按住」；不是「壓低薪資」那種常見用法。
generatedExample: The guard held down the prisoner until help arrived.

explanationZhTw: 「gripped」表示「緊緊抓住、牢牢握住」；在這裡帶有明顯的身體束縛感。
generatedExample: Her hand gripped the railing tightly.

explanationZhTw: 「gravely」在這裡不是「嚴重地」的醫療語氣，而是「神情凝重地、嚴肅地」。
generatedExample: He listened gravely to the warning.

explanationZhTw: 「coarse」指外表粗糙、不細緻；「worn」在這裡是「顯得疲憊、老舊、被歲月磨損」。合在一起是在形容臉部的滄桑感。
generatedExample: After years outdoors, his hands looked coarse and worn.

explanationZhTw: 「pouches」原意是「小袋、囊」，這裡指「眼袋、眼下的浮腫」。
generatedExample: She noticed pouches under her father's eyes after a sleepless night.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「face」不是「臉」，而是儀表或刻度盤的「表面、盤面」。屬於依上下文判讀的特殊意思。
generatedExample: The needle moved across the face of the gauge.

explanationZhTw: `meet` 的過去式片語，意思是「再次見面、再遇到」；這裡只是字面上的再次碰面，沒有特殊慣用法。
generatedExample: We met again at the train station after ten years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「淹沒」，而是指劇痛突然整個湧遍全身。
generatedExample: A wave of relief flooded her when she heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 指「致命的傷」，也就是嚴重到可能讓人死亡的傷勢。
generatedExample: The doctor said he had suffered a mortal injury in the crash.

explanationZhTw: 指被強力扭拉到變形。
generatedExample: The storm wrenched the metal gate out of shape.

explanationZhTw: 指被撕裂開、分開到幾乎碎掉。
generatedExample: The old tent was torn apart by the wind.

explanationZhTw: 指讓他冒汗，這裡是疼痛逼出冷汗。
generatedExample: The heat brought the sweat out on my face in minutes.

explanationZhTw: 指「快要斷裂」；在這裡是說他的脊骨似乎快要斷了。
generatedExample: The branch was about to snap under the weight of the snow.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指咬緊牙關、硬撐著忍痛或忍耐。
generatedExample: She set her teeth and finished the race.

explanationZhTw: especial 是較少見、較正式的說法，意思接近「特別的、格外的」。這裡的 especial fear 就是「特別害怕的事」。
generatedExample: Her especial concern was that the plan would fail at the last minute.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是字面意思「脊椎、脊柱」，不是比喻的「骨幹」。
generatedExample: He twisted awkwardly and hurt his backbone while lifting the box.

explanationZhTw: 指腦中非常清楚、具體、幾乎像真的一樣的畫面。
generatedExample: She had a vivid mental picture of the house after reading the description.

explanationZhTw: 指脊椎骨，也就是構成脊柱的一節一節骨頭。
generatedExample: A doctor can see whether the vertebrae are aligned correctly in the X-ray.

explanationZhTw: 指腦脊髓液，包圍並保護腦和脊髓的液體。
generatedExample: The lab tested the spinal fluid for signs of infection.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示「把某物往後拉／往後移開」。這裡是把控制桿往回拉，暗示動作乾脆而有力。
generatedExample: She drew back the curtain to let in more light.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「槓桿、拉桿」。在這裡是用來控制裝置的操作桿，不是抽象意思。
generatedExample: Pull the lever to start the machine.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是較具文學性的用法，將疼痛比喻成「一波」感覺，並用 receded 表示「退去、減弱」。不是字面上的海浪後退，而是痛感消退。
generatedExample: After the medicine started working, the wave of nausea receded.

explanationZhTw: `run up to` 表示「最高到達、上限是」。這裡不是字面上的「跑向」，而是說這個刻度最多到 100。
generatedExample: This meter runs up to 500 liters.

explanationZhTw: `throughout` 表示「整個期間、在整段過程中」。這裡強調在整段對話期間都要記住這件事。
generatedExample: Please keep this rule in mind throughout the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `have it in my power` 是慣用說法，表示「我有能力/權力去做某事」。這裡帶有威脅意味，指他有能力造成傷害。
generatedExample: It is in my power to approve your request today.

explanationZhTw: `prevaricate` 是較正式的字，意思是「閃爍其詞、故意含糊其辭、說謊避重就輕」。在這裡指不要用任何方式迴避真話。
generatedExample: The witness tried to prevaricate when asked about the missing money.

explanationZhTw: `fall below` 這裡不是單純「掉到下面」，而是指「低於平常水準、表現不如以往」。整句是在說連智力表現稍微變差都不行。
generatedExample: On a bad day, my concentration can fall below my normal level.

explanationZhTw: `said` 是 `say` 的過去式，意思是「說、講」。這裡表示 Winston 說了「Yes」。
generatedExample: She said she would come later.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「severe」指態度或表情很嚴厲、拘謹，不是天氣或疾病那種「嚴重」。
generatedExample: The teacher was severe, but fair.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「resettled」是把眼鏡重新扶正、調整好位置，不是「重新定居」。
generatedExample: She resettled her spectacles before reading the letter.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「air」是「神情、樣子、給人的印象」，不是空氣。
generatedExample: He had the air of someone who knew the answer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「anxious to」表示「急切想要、熱切希望」，不是「焦慮不安」。
generatedExample: She was anxious to finish the work early.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「費心處理、花力氣對付」的意思，不是單純的麻煩；語氣也帶有刻意關注某人的意味。
generatedExample: The teacher took trouble with the student and explained the rules twice.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「值得費心、值得多花工夫」，是評價某人或某事值得投入精力。
generatedExample: The old house was worth trouble because the view was excellent.

explanationZhTw: `fight against` 表示「對抗、抗拒」；在這裡是指他一直抗拒接受真相。
generatedExample: She fought against the temptation to check her phone.

explanationZhTw: `defective` 在這裡是「有缺陷的、功能不正常的」；整個片語指記憶有問題。
generatedExample: After the fall, he worried that he had a defective memory.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「說服別人」，而是「說服自己相信某件事」；常帶有自我欺騙的意思。
generatedExample: You cannot just persuade yourself that everything is fine.

explanationZhTw: `curable` 表示「可治療的、可痊癒的」。
generatedExample: The doctor said the infection is curable with antibiotics.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「意志力上的一點努力」；不是普通的體力勞動，而是靠自制力去做。
generatedExample: It took an effort of the will to stay quiet during the argument.

explanationZhTw: `cling to` 原意是「緊抓不放」，延伸為「執著於、死守著」某種想法或狀態。
generatedExample: He kept clinging to the old plan even after it failed.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，表示「誤以為、以為」；常暗示對方的理解不完全正確。
generatedExample: I was under the impression that the interview was tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 「arrested」是指「被逮捕、拘捕」，在這裡用的是一般常見的法律情境用法。
generatedExample: He was arrested after the police found the stolen car.

explanationZhTw: 「Oceania」在這裡不是地理上的大洋洲，而是小說中的虛構國家名稱，屬於情境中的特殊指涉。
generatedExample: In the novel, Oceania is controlled by a powerful government.

explanationZhTw: 「Eastasia」也是小說中的虛構國家名稱，不是現實世界中的地名。
generatedExample: The report said Eastasia had signed a new peace treaty.

explanationZhTw: 表示「與……處於戰爭狀態／交戰中」。這裡是政治與軍事語境中的固定用法，不是字面上單獨拆開理解的詞組。
generatedExample: The two countries have been at war with each other for decades.

explanationZhTw: 表示「倒抽一口氣；猛然吸氣」, 通常帶有驚訝、緊張或害怕的語氣。
generatedExample: She drew in her breath when she saw the broken window.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無法把目光從……移開」；強調一直盯著看，捨不得或無法分心。
generatedExample: I could not take my eyes away from the screen.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 dial 不是動詞「撥號」，而是名詞，指裝置上的刻度盤、轉盤或儀表盤。
generatedExample: He turned the dial to make the radio louder.

explanationZhTw: 「真相；事實」。這裡指的是被要求說出的、被認可的事實。
generatedExample: She finally told the truth about what happened.

explanationZhTw: 「你的真相／你所認定的真相」。這裡不是單純指客觀事實，而是帶有主觀、被逼問式的語氣，和「真正的事實」形成對比。
generatedExample: Everyone has their own truth, but we still need the facts.

explanationZhTw: 「arrested」是「被逮捕」的意思。這裡暗示敘述者的個人命運和政治事件直接連在一起。
generatedExample: The suspect was arrested outside the station.

explanationZhTw: 「at war with」表示「與……處於戰爭狀態」。這裡是字面意義，但也帶出敘述者所說的戰爭立場突然改變，強調政治宣傳式的矛盾感。
generatedExample: The two countries were at war with each other for many years.

explanationZhTw: 「in alliance with」表示「和……結盟、同盟」。這是正式用語，常見於政治、軍事或商業合作情境。
generatedExample: The smaller nation formed an alliance with its neighbors for defense.

explanationZhTw: 「lasted」表示「持續了」。這裡不是一般的「保留」或「耐久」，而是指戰爭狀態持續了一段時間。
generatedExample: The meeting lasted for two hours.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「stopped him」不是單純指「把他停住」，而是指用動作制止他繼續說話或做下去，也就是「打斷／示意他別再繼續」。
generatedExample: She stopped him before he could reveal the secret.

explanationZhTw: 指「妄想、錯誤卻深信不疑的觀念」；這裡是精神狀態上的錯誤信念，不是一般誤會。
generatedExample: He was trapped in a delusion that everyone was lying to him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `one-time` 不是「一次的」，而是「曾經的、前任的」；整體意思是「曾經是黨員的人」。
generatedExample: She met a one-time colleague who now worked in another city.

explanationZhTw: 指「處決、執行死刑」；不是一般的「執行某事」，而是司法或政治上的致死處置。
generatedExample: The spy was executed after a closed trial.

explanationZhTw: 指「背叛、叛變」；通常帶有對群體、國家或信任關係的嚴重背叛意味。
generatedExample: He was accused of treachery by his former allies.

explanationZhTw: 指「蓄意破壞、暗中搞破壞」；常用於政治、工業或軍事情境。
generatedExample: They were arrested for sabotage of the railway system.

explanationZhTw: 指「供詞、坦白認罪」；這裡是向當局承認罪行的說法。
generatedExample: Her confession changed the direction of the investigation.

explanationZhTw: 法律用語，指「被指控犯有」；`charge` 在此不是一般的「收費」或「充電」。
generatedExample: She was charged with fraud last year.

explanationZhTw: 指「書面或文件證據」；不是紀錄片，而是能支持事實的正式資料。
generatedExample: The lawyer presented documentary evidence to support the claim.

explanationZhTw: 指「幻覺」；在這裡表示他錯誤地感知到自己曾經看到或拿過那張照片。
generatedExample: He said the bright lights caused a brief hallucination.

explanationZhTw: 偶然遇見、無意間發現；這裡指他多年以前意外看到那張照片。
generatedExample: I chanced upon an old bookstore while walking home.

explanationZhTw: 看不見了；表示那東西又離開他的視線。
generatedExample: The bus disappeared out of sight around the corner.

explanationZhTw: 猛拉、硬扯；這裡不是「扳手」名詞，而是用力把身體扯開。
generatedExample: She wrenched the window open with both hands.

explanationZhTw: 公分；這裡用來強調「連一點點距離都動不了」。
generatedExample: The shelf is two centimetres too low.

explanationZhTw: 旋鈕、刻度盤；這裡指裝置上的控制部件，不是「撥號」的意思。
generatedExample: Turn the dial slowly until the radio is clear.

explanationZhTw: 「exist」表示「存在」；這裡是一般但重要的字，用來說某件事真實存在。
generatedExample: Many people believe the old legend still exists.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「cried」不是指「哭」，而是指「大聲喊道／叫道」；在敘述對話時很常見。
generatedExample: ‘Help!’ she cried when she saw the fire.

explanationZhTw: 「memory hole」在這裡不是字面上的「記憶洞」，而是特定制度中的銷毀通道或入口，用來把文件丟進去讓它被焚毀或抹除，屬於情境中很特殊的用法。
generatedExample: The clerk dropped the report into the memory hole before anyone could read it.

explanationZhTw: 「lifted」在這裡是把某個有格柵的蓋子往上掀開；「grating」指格柵狀蓋板或鐵柵，屬於描述具體動作與物件的用法。
generatedExample: She lifted the grating to check the drain below.

explanationZhTw: 「whirling away」表示快速地旋轉、飄轉著離開；這裡強調紙張被氣流帶動的動態畫面，屬於片語式的動作表達。
generatedExample: The leaves were whirling away in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 「in a flash of flame」字面是「在一瞬間的火焰閃光中」，這裡用來表示紙張很快就被火吞沒，帶有強烈畫面感，屬於語境中的特殊表達。
generatedExample: The note was vanishing in a flash of flame before he could react.

explanationZhTw: 「turned away」是常見片語動詞，意思是轉身離開、把身體和注意力從某處移開。
generatedExample: When the argument started, he turned away and left the room.

explanationZhTw: 指燃燒後剩下的灰燼；這裡是字面意思，不是比喻。
generatedExample: After the fire, only ashes remained in the fireplace.

explanationZhTw: 可辨認的；這裡強調連「像灰燼都難以辨認」的程度，表示已經非常模糊、無法確認。
generatedExample: The fingerprints were no longer identifiable after the rain.

explanationZhTw: 存在；這裡表示「根本不存在」。
generatedExample: For them, that problem does not exist.

explanationZhTw: 從來不存在；這裡用來加強否定，表示不只是現在沒有，而是一直都沒有。
generatedExample: The agreement never existed, so there was nothing to cancel.

explanationZhTw: 「exist」指「存在」；這裡是在強調某件事確實存在，不只是被提到而已。
generatedExample: The old photo still exists in my wallet.

explanationZhTw: 「exist」指「存在」；這裡是在強調某件事確實存在，不只是被提到而已。
generatedExample: The old photo still exists in my wallet.

explanationZhTw: 「exist」指「存在」；這裡是在強調某件事確實存在，不只是被提到而已。

explanationZhTw: 「memory」在這裡是「記憶、腦海中的印象」，不是電腦的記憶體。

explanationZhTw: 「remember」是「記得、想起」，用來表示腦中保有或喚起某個資訊。

explanationZhTw: 「remember」是「記得、想起」，用來表示腦中保有或喚起某個資訊。
generatedExample: I remember meeting her at the station.

explanationZhTw: 「remember」是常見的 A2 字彙，這裡表示「記得；回想起」。整句是在說「我不記得那件事」。
generatedExample: I remember your name, but I don't remember where we met.

explanationZhTw: 表示突然感到沮喪、失望或不安。
generatedExample: My heart sank when I realized I had missed the deadline.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡的造詞，指同時接受兩個彼此矛盾的想法，卻不覺得衝突。
generatedExample: The regime relied on doublethink to keep people obedient.

explanationZhTw: 無助感；覺得自己完全無法改變局面。
generatedExample: After the error spread online, she felt a deep helplessness.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「否認」的名詞用法，指先前曾否認自己記得那件事。
generatedExample: His denial of the accusation sounded rehearsed.

explanationZhTw: 欺騙手法；騙術。
generatedExample: The magician's trickery made the audience gasp.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「瘋子般的脫臼」，而是指思緒或認知嚴重錯亂、脫節的狀態。
generatedExample: The shock caused a strange dislocation in the mind that he could not explain.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，表示帶著推測、揣摩的態度；這裡是指奧布萊恩一邊低頭看他，一邊像在判斷或猜測他的反應。
generatedExample: She studied the report speculatively before asking any questions.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，意思是「看起來像／有……的樣子」；用來形容某人的神態或氣質很像某種角色。
generatedExample: He had the air of someone who knew exactly what he was doing.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「費心、格外用心、盡力而為」；這裡指老師很認真地照顧和教導那個孩子。
generatedExample: She took pains to make the instructions clear for everyone.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，指「任性的、難管教的、行為不按常規的」；在這裡形容孩子不太聽話。
generatedExample: The wayward student ignored the rules and left early.

explanationZhTw: slogan 指「標語、口號」，這裡是政黨用來傳達立場的簡短宣傳語。
generatedExample: The campaign slogan was simple and memorable.

explanationZhTw: dealing with 在這裡不是「處理」某物，而是表示「談到、涉及、關於」。
generatedExample: The lesson is dealing with climate change.

explanationZhTw: if you please 是較正式、帶禮貌色彩的說法，相當於「請」或「勞駕」。
generatedExample: Close the window, if you please.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 controls 不是單純「操作」某個東西，而是指「左右、掌控」；在這句口號裡，重點是誰能掌控敘述，就能影響過去、現在和未來的理解方式。

explanationZhTw: obediently 表示「順從地、聽話地」；這裡帶有諷刺感，表示溫斯頓只是乖乖照著別人的話重複。
generatedExample: The child nodded obediently and followed the teacher's instructions.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 controls 表示「掌控、左右」，不是單純操作，而是能影響事情的走向。

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 controls 表示「掌控、左右」，不是單純操作，而是能影響事情的走向。

explanationZhTw: approval 在這裡是「認可、贊同」的意思。
generatedExample: She gave a quiet nod of approval after the presentation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 real existence 不是一般的「存在」，而是帶有哲學意味的「真實存在」或「是否真的存在」。
generatedExample: The debate was about whether freedom has real existence or is only an idea.

explanationZhTw: 「無助感」；指無法控制局勢、也無力改變結果的狀態。
generatedExample: After the car broke down in the storm, she felt helplessness for a moment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示某種情緒或狀態突然籠罩、壓倒某人。
generatedExample: A strange silence descended upon the room after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面『飛來飛去』，而是指視線快速地移向某處。
generatedExample: Her eyes flitted towards the door when she heard the noise.

explanationZhTw: 此處指裝置上的旋鈕或刻度盤，不是一般日常說的『撥號』。
generatedExample: Turn the dial to make the radio louder.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 save 是『使免於』，意思是避免他受到痛苦。
generatedExample: Wearing a coat saved me from the cold wind.

explanationZhTw: 表示「微弱地、若有若無地」；這裡不是大笑，而是很輕的笑意。
generatedExample: She smiled faintly and said nothing.

explanationZhTw: 指「形而上學家／研究存在、本體等哲學問題的人」；這裡帶有嘲諷語氣。
generatedExample: The metaphysician spent years thinking about what reality really is.

explanationZhTw: 指「存在、實存」；在這裡是哲學上的抽象概念，不只是一般的「活著」。
generatedExample: They began to doubt the existence of the old city.

explanationZhTw: 表示「更準確地說、說得更明確一點」；用來修正或收窄前面的說法。
generatedExample: Let me put it more precisely so there is no confusion.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「具體地、以實體方式」的意思，和抽象的想法相對。
generatedExample: Can you show how this would work concretely in real life?

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「某個地方、反正有個不確定的地方」；重點是不知道或不想精確指出位置。
generatedExample: I left my notebook somewhere or other in the house.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `No` 是直接否定或拒絕的簡短回答，意思是「不／不要／沒有」。
generatedExample: "No," she said quietly, and closed the door.

explanationZhTw: 「if at all」表示「如果真的有的話；即使有也幾乎沒有」，用來強調前面的情況非常不確定。這裡是在質疑「過去」是否真的存在。
generatedExample: I might go to the party tonight, if at all.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`records` 指「記錄、檔案、正式存檔的資料」，不是單純泛指一般的紀錄動作。
generatedExample: The decision was preserved in the company records.

explanationZhTw: `written down` 是片語動詞 `write down` 的過去分詞形式，意思是「寫下來、記錄下來」。
generatedExample: Please write down the address before we leave.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `records` 不是「唱片」或一般「紀錄」，而是指「檔案、文件記錄」，也就是官方保存的資料。
generatedExample: The clerk checked the records before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `mind` 指的是「心智、腦海、思想」；不是動詞 `mind`（介意、在乎）。
generatedExample: The answer was already in her mind before he asked the question.

explanationZhTw: `memories` 指「記憶、回憶」；這裡是指存在於人的記憶之中。
generatedExample: Some places stay alive in our memories for years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「記憶、腦中保留的印象」；整句很短，意思是把事情記在心裡或回想起來。
generatedExample: In memory, I can still picture the old house by the sea.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「掌控、支配」；不是單純管理，而是有權力決定內容和方向。
generatedExample: The pilot can control the plane with both hands on the wheel.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 records 指「官方檔案、紀錄文件」；不是唱片，也不是一般私人的記錄。
generatedExample: The city keeps records of every building permit.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「掌控、支配」；不是單純管理，而是有權力決定內容和方向。

explanationZhTw: 這裡是複數，指「許多記憶、回憶」；在這個語境中也暗示人們對過去的認知。
generatedExample: Her memories of childhood are full of details.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 the past 指「過去的歷史事實、曾經發生的一切」；語氣上比單純的時間上的過去更具政治意味。
generatedExample: People often look back at the past to understand the present.

explanationZhTw: 表示「短暫地、片刻地」，強調時間很短。
generatedExample: I was momentarily confused by the question.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指儀器上的旋鈕或刻度盤，不是「撥號」的意思。
generatedExample: He turned the dial until the room felt warmer.

explanationZhTw: 表示「非自願的；不由自主的」，是自己無法刻意控制的反應。
generatedExample: Blinking is an involuntary action.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示某件事不受自己掌控，像是超出個人意志之外。
generatedExample: Her fear felt outside herself, as if it came from nowhere she could control.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 controlled 不是單純「控制器材」，而是指「支配、約束、掌控」自己的記憶。
generatedExample: He said no one had controlled his choices for years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `manner` 不是「禮貌」的意思，而是指一個人的神態、態度或舉止；整句是在說奧布萊恩的神情又變得嚴厲起來。
generatedExample: Her manner became tense when the subject was mentioned.

explanationZhTw: `stern` 在這裡表示「嚴厲的、冷峻的」，形容他的表情或態度變得不再溫和。
generatedExample: The teacher gave us a stern warning.

explanationZhTw: 「相反地」；用來直接反駁前一句，表示意思完全相反。
generatedExample: On the contrary, I think the plan is too risky.

explanationZhTw: 「自律」；指控制自己的行為、情緒和慾望。
generatedExample: Success often depends on self-discipline.

explanationZhTw: 「屈服／順從的行為」；這裡指主動接受對方要求。
generatedExample: He saw apology as an act of submission.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「理智的代價」；這裡表示為了保持正常、理智必須付出的代價。
generatedExample: For her, rest was the price of sanity.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，指「只有自己一個人持那種看法的人」；強調孤立無援。
generatedExample: He felt like a minority of one in the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「有紀律的頭腦」；指能控制自己、保持理性思考的心智。
generatedExample: A disciplined mind helps you stay focused.

explanationZhTw: 「客觀的」；不受個人想法或感受影響。
generatedExample: We need an objective view of the problem.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「本身就算一個獨立存在／有其自身價值」。
generatedExample: She is a talented writer in her own right.

explanationZhTw: 「不言自明的」；指一看就像是真的、似乎不用證明。
generatedExample: The answer seemed self-evident to everyone.

explanationZhTw: 「自欺欺人」；讓自己相信不真實或錯誤的事。
generatedExample: Don't delude yourself into thinking it will be easy.

explanationZhTw: 「很快消亡／滅亡」；這裡指個人心智終會消失。
generatedExample: Without care, the memory of the event soon perishes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「認定／宣稱為」的意思，表示某事被當作真理。
generatedExample: The committee holds the proposal to be necessary.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「站在……的角度看」或「用……的觀點理解」。
generatedExample: Try looking through the eyes of the customer.

explanationZhTw: 口語中表示「必須、一定要」；語氣比一般的 `must` 更口語。
generatedExample: I’ve got to finish this before noon.

explanationZhTw: 「自我摧毀的行為」；這裡指為了改變自己而進行的徹底自我否定。
generatedExample: Quitting his medicine was an act of self-destruction.

explanationZhTw: 「使自己謙卑／放低姿態」；不是單純謙虛，而是主動壓低自我。
generatedExample: You may need to humble yourself and ask for help.

explanationZhTw: 「理智正常的」；指心理狀態健全、思考正常。
generatedExample: After a break, I finally felt sane again.

explanationZhTw: 「sink in」在這裡不是指「沉下去」，而是指「慢慢被理解、逐漸領會」。整句表示他停頓一下，像是在讓剛剛說的話有時間被聽者消化吸收。
generatedExample: The news took a while to sink in, but then everyone understood the situation.

explanationZhTw: 「繼續說下去」；這裡表示說話沒有中斷，接著前面的話繼續講。
generatedExample: She went on explaining the rule until everyone understood.

explanationZhTw: 「日記」；用來每天記錄想法、事件或感受的本子。
generatedExample: I write one short note in my diary before bed.

explanationZhTw: 「自由」；這裡特別指能夠誠實表達、說出真話的自由。
generatedExample: People value the freedom to speak openly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是字面上的算術句子，但在文脈中用來表示「明顯無可否認的真理」或「基本事實」。
generatedExample: For him, the evidence was simple: two plus two make four.

explanationZhTw: 「held up」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是把手抬起、舉起來。
generatedExample: She held up her hand to ask a question.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「back」不是一般的「背部」，而是指「手背、手的背面」。
generatedExample: He wrote his phone number on the back of his hand.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「extended」表示「伸直、張開」，描述手指被伸直的狀態。
generatedExample: She extended her arm to reach the book.

explanationZhTw: 「fingers」是指手指，屬於基礎常用詞。
generatedExample: She counted the fingers on her left hand.

explanationZhTw: 「holding up」在這裡是「舉起、拿著讓人看見」的意思，不是「支撐」或「延遲」的用法。
generatedExample: Can you hold up the picture so everyone can see it?

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「the party」不是一般指派對，而是指《一九八四》中的執政黨／極權政黨，屬於特定語境下的用法。
generatedExample: In the novel, the party controls what people are allowed to say.

explanationZhTw: `four` 是數字「四」，這裡是直接用來回答數量或編號的簡短答句；屬於基礎常用詞。
generatedExample: There are four chairs around the table.

explanationZhTw: 「gasp」是突然倒抽一口氣，通常因疼痛、驚訝或喘不過氣。這裡指痛到吸氣的反射動作。
generatedExample: She gave a gasp when the cold water touched her skin.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「needle」是儀表上的指針，不是一般的針；用來表示數值位置。
generatedExample: The needle moved quickly as the temperature rose.

explanationZhTw: 「shot up」表示突然快速上升或飆高，常用來描述數值、價格或溫度。
generatedExample: Prices shot up after the storm.

explanationZhTw: 「sprung out」在這裡是突然冒出、湧出的意思，形容汗一下子大量出現。
generatedExample: Sweat sprang out on his forehead during the race.

explanationZhTw: 「clenching his teeth」是咬緊牙關，表示忍住疼痛或不舒服。這裡是固定的忍耐說法。
generatedExample: He kept working, clenching his teeth through the pain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「extended」不是「延長」，而是指手指仍然伸出、保持張開。
generatedExample: She held her arm extended so the doctor could check it.

explanationZhTw: 「drew back」是往後拉回、退回；這裡指把拉桿往後拉。
generatedExample: When the dog barked, he drew back a little.

explanationZhTw: 「lever」是槓桿、拉桿或操作桿，這裡指用來控制機器的拉桿。
generatedExample: Pull the lever to start the machine.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「eased」是減輕、緩和，特別是指疼痛變得比較不那麼強。
generatedExample: The medicine eased my headache within an hour.

explanationZhTw: fingers 是「手指」；這裡是字面意思，指手上的手指。
generatedExample: She held up three fingers to show the answer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 "needle" 不是縫衣針，而是儀表上的指針。
generatedExample: The needle on the gauge moved to the right.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 "went up" 表示「上升、增加」，不是單純的「走上去」。
generatedExample: The temperature went up after noon.

explanationZhTw: 「fingers」是「手指」的意思，屬於基礎名詞。這裡是在問溫斯頓有幾根手指，字面上很直接。
generatedExample: She counted the fingers on her left hand.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是修辭性的反問，意思是「我已經沒別的可說了」或「事情就是這樣」。在上下文中，它表達無奈、強調或收尾，而不是在真的提問。
generatedExample: I checked the report twice, and what else can I say? It was accurate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `needle` 不是縫衣針，而是儀表上的指針，表示數值在變化。
generatedExample: The needle on the gauge moved up quickly.

explanationZhTw: `filled his vision` 是說這些影像幾乎占滿了他的視野，讓他難以注意別的東西。
generatedExample: The bright lights filled her vision when she stepped outside.

explanationZhTw: `like pillars` 是比喻，表示手指又直又粗大，像柱子一樣。
generatedExample: The old trees stood like pillars along the road.

explanationZhTw: 「go on」在這裡是「繼續下去／撐下去」的意思，表示某人還能接著做、接著承受某件事。
generatedExample: I was tired, but I had to go on until the work was done.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「手指」。這裡是字面用法，屬於基礎常用字，適合 A2 以上學習者。
generatedExample: She counted her fingers after closing the box.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「no use」不是字面上的「沒有用途」，而是慣用說法，表示「沒用、無效、行不通」。
generatedExample: Arguing with him is no use.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「lying」是動詞 `lie` 的現在分詞，意思是「說謊」；在這句話中是直接指控對方不誠實。
generatedExample: She accused him of lying about the report.

explanationZhTw: 這是日常名詞，意思是「手指」；在這裡用在一個簡短的提問中，語氣帶有測試或盤問的感覺。
generatedExample: Count your fingers after you wash your hands.

explanationZhTw: pain：這裡指身體上的疼痛，語氣很急，表示說話者只想立刻停止折磨。
generatedExample: The pain in his leg got worse after he ran too far.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純『失去意識的狀態』的字面感，而是指『昏過去、暈倒』。
generatedExample: She lost consciousness after the accident and woke up in the hospital.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 bonds 不是一般的『關係、聯繫』，而是指綁住身體的『束縛、繩索』。
generatedExample: The prisoner tried to break the bonds around his wrists.

explanationZhTw: 指因寒冷或害怕而牙齒打顫，不是真的在說牙齒會『聊天』。
generatedExample: We stood in the snow with our teeth chattering.

explanationZhTw: 表示『緊緊抱住、依附著』，帶有求助或依賴的感覺。
generatedExample: The child clung to her mother during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 指『保護者』，這裡帶有強烈的安全感與被守護的意味。
generatedExample: My older brother was my protector when I was young.

explanationZhTw: 指「學得比較慢的人」；這裡不是在描述一般的速度，而是在評價 Winston 需要較久才理解或吸收。
generatedExample: She is a slow learner, but she improves with practice.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「我怎麼幫忙」，而是慣用法，意思接近「我也沒辦法／我無法阻止」。
generatedExample: How can I help it if the train is delayed?

explanationZhTw: 指一邊哭、一邊含糊不清地說話；常帶有很委屈、鼻音很重的語氣。
generatedExample: The child blubbered after losing her toy.

explanationZhTw: 表示「就在我眼前、我親眼看到」，不是只是在描述位置而已。
generatedExample: The accident happened right in front of my eyes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純數學敘述，而是用來強調「事實明明白白、無可否認」。
generatedExample: With the evidence laid out, two and two are four.

explanationZhTw: 「at once」在這裡是慣用法，表示「同時、一起」。不是指「立刻」；整句是在說它們會同時是全部。
generatedExample: All three alarms went off at once.

explanationZhTw: 「try harder」表示「更加努力、再更用力嘗試」。這裡是在催促對方加強表現或努力。
generatedExample: If you want to pass, you must try harder this week.

explanationZhTw: 「sane」表示「理智的、精神正常的」。在這裡是較正式的用法，意思是恢復正常的心智狀態。
generatedExample: After a long day of bad news, it was hard to stay sane.

explanationZhTw: 逐漸消退；這裡指疼痛像潮水退去一樣慢慢變弱。
generatedExample: After a few minutes, the anger ebbed away and he could speak calmly.

explanationZhTw: 用頭示意；不是指「移動」東西，而是用頭部做手勢來表示意思。
generatedExample: She motioned with her head toward the exit.

explanationZhTw: 一動不動的；形容完全沒有移動。
generatedExample: The statue looked so real that it seemed almost immobile.

explanationZhTw: 進行中的程序／過程；這裡不是「法庭訴訟程序」，而是指整個醫療檢查的進行。
generatedExample: The doctor watched the proceedings carefully before speaking.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 Again 不是單純「再次」的時間副詞，而是作為獨立呼語，意思是「再說一次／重複一遍」。
generatedExample: Again, please say that more slowly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 needle 不是「針」，而是儀表上的指針或刻度指標，用來顯示數值。
generatedExample: The needle on the meter moved into the red zone.

explanationZhTw: spasm 指突然的痙攣、抽搐或一陣劇痛；這裡是說只要撐到那陣痛過去就好。
generatedExample: She waited for the spasm in her back to pass.

explanationZhTw: ceased to notice 表示「已經不再注意」；這裡強調痛到連自己有沒有在叫都不在意了。
generatedExample: After the long meeting, I ceased to notice the noise around me.

explanationZhTw: draw back 是片語動詞，表示「往後拉回」；這裡是把控制桿往後拉。
generatedExample: He drew back the curtain and let the light in.

explanationZhTw: lever 指槓桿、控制桿，這裡是機器或裝置上的操作桿。
generatedExample: Pull the lever to start the machine.

explanationZhTw: 「手指」；這裡是最基本的身體部位詞彙，用來詢問有幾根手指。在這句話中，它也帶出一種直接、審問式的語氣。
generatedExample: How many fingers can you see on my left hand?

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「我想／我猜」，語氣是不太確定的判斷，不是強烈肯定。
generatedExample: I suppose we can leave now.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `trying` 表示「努力嘗試」，`try to do` 是常見結構，意思是刻意想做到某件事。
generatedExample: She is trying to finish the report before lunch.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「想要、選擇」的意思，語氣比口語的 want 更正式。
generatedExample: Which color do you wish to choose for the cover?

explanationZhTw: 說服；使對方接受某個想法或做法。
generatedExample: She persuaded me to join the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 作副詞，表示「真的、確實」；這裡用來加強語氣，表示說話者很想見到他們。
generatedExample: I really want to see them again.

explanationZhTw: 「intermittently」在這裡不是單純指「有時候」，而是表示記憶和意識斷斷續續地出現。
generatedExample: She intermittently forgot why she had come into the room.

explanationZhTw: 「screwed-up」在這裡是口語用法，表示眼皮用力擠緊、緊閉，不是字面上的「擰壞」。
generatedExample: He kept his screwed-up eyelids shut against the bright light.

explanationZhTw: 這是隱喻，把很多手指形容成「一片森林」，強調數量多、視覺混亂。
generatedExample: From a distance, a forest of umbrellas filled the square.

explanationZhTw: 「weaving in and out」在這裡表示來回穿梭、交錯移動，常用來描述動作很複雜或很難分辨。
generatedExample: The runners were weaving in and out through the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示彼此互相遮住、看不清楚，強調畫面一直變動。
generatedExample: The cars disappeared behind one another as the road curved.

explanationZhTw: 「reappearing again」表示原本看不見的東西又再次出現，帶有重複浮現的感覺。
generatedExample: The same image kept reappearing again in my mind.

explanationZhTw: 「impossible」在這裡強調不是單純困難，而是完全無法完成。
generatedExample: It was impossible to hear her voice over the noise.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「identity」不是「身分」，而是指「相同／等同」的抽象概念；整個片語帶有神祕、哲學式的用法。
generatedExample: The teacher explained the mysterious identity between the two patterns.

explanationZhTw: 「died down」是片語動詞，表示逐漸減弱、慢慢平息。
generatedExample: The rain died down after midnight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是字面動作，但在敘述中帶出「恢復視線後仍看到同樣景象」的效果。
generatedExample: She opened her eyes and looked at the clock.

explanationZhTw: 「innumerable」表示多到數不清，語氣比 many 更強。
generatedExample: There were innumerable stars in the night sky.

explanationZhTw: 「streaming past」在這裡不是「串流播放」，而是指東西像流水一樣不停經過。
generatedExample: People were streaming past the station entrance.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示反覆交叉來回，突出混亂、重複的動態畫面。
generatedExample: The lines were crossing and recrossing over the page.

explanationZhTw: 「shut his eyes」是常見說法，表示把眼睛閉上；這裡也延續了他試圖逃避眼前景象的動作。
generatedExample: He shut his eyes and waited for the sound to pass.

explanationZhTw: 「fingers」是「手指」；這裡屬於基礎詞彙，但在整句中是理解問題的關鍵名詞。
generatedExample: She counted her fingers after closing her hand.

explanationZhTw: 「hold up」在這裡不是「舉高」的抽象意思，而是指「把手舉起來讓人看、展示」。
generatedExample: Hold up three fingers so I can count them.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `kill` 是字面義，意思是「殺死、害死」。整句是在說「你再那樣做一次，我就會被你害死」。
generatedExample: If you push me into the water again, you might kill me.

explanationZhTw: `in all honesty` 是常見片語，意思是「老實說／說真的」，用來強調接下來是誠實的回答。
generatedExample: In all honesty, I don't think this plan will work.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `better` 是比較級，意思是「比較好」或「好一些」。在這句對話裡，它是省略式回應，表示某件事已改善或比較理想。
generatedExample: “How are you feeling?” “Better,” she said after taking a rest.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指一種極度舒適、像被治癒一樣的暖意，不只是一般的「溫暖」。
generatedExample: After the long hike, he felt a blissful, healing warmth from the soup.

explanationZhTw: 指「只記得一半、幾乎快忘了」；這裡強調疼痛已經退到不太清楚的程度。
generatedExample: By morning, the argument was half-forgotten.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「抬頭看」。
generatedExample: She looked up when her name was called.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「一看見……就」；常用來表示看到某人或某物時立刻產生反應。
generatedExample: At sight of the storm clouds, we hurried inside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「翻轉」，而是指心中突然湧起強烈、複雜的感受。
generatedExample: When he saw the letter, his heart seemed to turn over.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「歸根究柢／本質上」。
generatedExample: At bottom, they both wanted the same thing.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示把人逼到「快要發瘋」的邊緣。
generatedExample: The constant pressure drove her to the edge of lunacy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般「熟人」，而是指非常親近、彼此了解很深的人。
generatedExample: After years of working together, they became intimates.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在某個不確定的地方」；這裡帶有模糊、難以明說的意味。
generatedExample: I left my keys somewhere or other on the desk.

explanationZhTw: 指「像聊天一樣自然輕鬆的語氣」，不是正式或生硬的口吻。
generatedExample: She explained the policy in a conversational tone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不只是「你在哪裡」，也可以帶出「你現在身在什麼處境、位置或狀態中」的意味，屬於語境上的用法。
generatedExample: After the long meeting, I wasn't sure where I was in the discussion.

explanationZhTw: `guess` 表示「猜、推測」。這裡不是隨便亂猜，而是根據已知線索做判斷。
generatedExample: I can guess the answer from the clues.

explanationZhTw: `Ministry of Love` 是《一九八四》裡的機構名稱，帶有反諷意味。字面上像「愛的部會」，實際上卻和控制、折磨有關。
generatedExample: In the Ministry of Love, even silence felt dangerous.

explanationZhTw: 「how long」在這裡是問「持續多久／過了多久」；不是問長度，而是問時間經過的期間。
generatedExample: Do you know how long she has been waiting?

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `imagine` 不是指「想像出畫面」，而是「以為、認為」；也就是在問對方為什麼會這樣推測。
generatedExample: I imagine they will arrive later than expected.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「供認、坦承（罪行或秘密）」，不是單純的「承認意見」；常見於刑事、審問或逼供的語境。
generatedExample: After hours of questioning, he finally confessed to taking the money.

explanationZhTw: 「reason」在這裡是「原因、理由」的意思。句子是在否定對方先前提出的解釋，表示真正的原因不是那個。
generatedExample: The reason I was late was heavy traffic.

explanationZhTw: 「punish」指「懲罰、處罰」。這裡是在說某人做某事的目的，是要對「他們」施以懲罰。
generatedExample: The teacher decided to punish the students for breaking the rules.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `extract` 不是單純「提取」，而是「逼出、迫使供出」；整個片語表示逼人招供。
generatedExample: They tried to extract a confession by questioning him for hours.

explanationZhTw: `cure` 在這裡不是治好身體疾病，而是指「把你矯正／改造成他們認可的樣子」。
generatedExample: The program claims it can cure bad habits in just one month.

explanationZhTw: `sane` 指「精神正常的、理智的」；在這裡帶有政治語境，暗示讓你接受他們的現實定義。
generatedExample: After the crisis, she needed time to feel sane again.

explanationZhTw: `overt act` 指「外顯的行為、表面可見的行動」，和內在想法相對。
generatedExample: The law focuses on the overt act, not just the intention behind it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `change` 不是一般的「改變」，而是指徹底改造一個人的思想與立場。
generatedExample: The training is designed to change them, not just teach them new skills.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「看起來巨大得不成比例」的意思，因為距離很近而產生的視覺效果，不只是單純的「很大」。
generatedExample: The tower looked enormous when I stood right under it.

explanationZhTw: 指「接近、距離很近」；這裡強調距離造成的視覺變形效果。
generatedExample: The photograph lost detail because of the camera's nearness to the object.

explanationZhTw: 指「非常、極度地」醜陋，帶有強烈負面感受；這裡不是一般描述，而是加重語氣。
generatedExample: The old statue was hideously ugly after years of damage.

explanationZhTw: exaltation 在這裡表示一種「高亢、亢奮、近乎狂熱的激昂感」，不是日常較少見的「提升」或宗教語感。
generatedExample: She spoke with a strange exaltation that made everyone uneasy.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻用法，表示「突然感到害怕、沮喪或退縮」，不是字面上的心臟縮小。
generatedExample: My heart shrank when I heard the bad news.

explanationZhTw: 指「因害怕而縮起身子、畏縮」，常用來描述人躲避威脅時的姿態。
generatedExample: The child cowered behind the sofa during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「純粹出於惡意、毫無正當理由地」；wantonness 是較少見的正式詞，強調任性且帶破壞性的故意行為。
generatedExample: He ruined the game out of sheer wantonness.

explanationZhTw: 表示「轉身離開、把身體或注意力轉向別處」；這裡是動作描寫，不是單純的方向改變。
generatedExample: She turned away before anyone could see her crying.

explanationZhTw: 表示「來回走了幾步」，常用來描寫焦躁、思考或等待時的踱步。
generatedExample: He took a pace or two up and down while he waited for the answer.

explanationZhTw: 指「激烈地、強烈地、帶著情緒地」；這裡是語氣明顯變弱之前的描述。
generatedExample: She vehemently denied the accusation.

explanationZhTw: 「martyrdoms」在這裡指「殉道／成為烈士的情況」；也就是某人因信念受害而被塑造成烈士的事件。
generatedExample: The regime wanted no martyrdoms, so it kept the executions secret.

explanationZhTw: 「Inquisition」是「宗教裁判所」的歷史名稱，指用來審查和懲罰異端的機構。
generatedExample: The Inquisition became a symbol of religious intolerance.

explanationZhTw: 「eradicate」在這裡是「徹底根除」的意思。
generatedExample: The new policy aimed to eradicate corruption.

explanationZhTw: 「heresy」指「異端信仰」或「異端邪說」，也就是被主流宗教視為錯誤的觀點。
generatedExample: In that era, even small differences in belief could be called heresy.

explanationZhTw: 「perpetuating」是「使持續存在、延續下去」；這裡強調反而讓異端更長久。
generatedExample: Old habits can be perpetuating inequality without anyone noticing.

explanationZhTw: 「at any rate」是口語常用語，意思是「無論如何、至少」。
generatedExample: I may be late, but at any rate I will be there tonight.

explanationZhTw: 「wore them down」是片語動詞，意思是「把某人折磨得筋疲力盡、意志被消磨殆盡」。
generatedExample: The constant pressure wore her down until she gave up.

explanationZhTw: 「put into their mouths」在這裡不是字面上的「放進嘴裡」，而是指「強迫他們照別人塞給他們的內容招供／發言」。
generatedExample: The witness said the confession had been put into his mouth by the police.

explanationZhTw: 「posterity」指「後世、後代、未來世代」。
generatedExample: She wrote the notes for posterity, not for immediate praise.

explanationZhTw: 「vindicate」在這裡是「證明某人是對的、洗刷冤屈」；說話者是在否定這種可能。
generatedExample: The documents later vindicated his decision.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，意思是「被徹底從歷史中抹去，連痕跡都不留下」。
generatedExample: Without records, their names were lifted clean out from the stream of history.

explanationZhTw: 「annihilated」表示「徹底毀滅、完全消滅」。
generatedExample: The village was annihilated by the earthquake.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「費心、費事」的意思，常用在口語裡，表示覺得某件事值得做或不值得做。
generatedExample: Why bother to call if you are already outside?

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「折磨」的字面意思，也可延伸為讓人非常痛苦或難受。
generatedExample: The loud noise tortured me all night.

explanationZhTw: 表示「短暫的、片刻的」，常用來描述情緒或狀態只維持一下子。
generatedExample: She felt a momentary panic before the interview.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「檢查」，而是指他突然放慢腳步、停頓一下。
generatedExample: He checked his step when he heard his name called.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「說出、發出」的正式用法，常指把話講出口。
generatedExample: She uttered a few words and then went silent.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「造成最小差異」，而是「完全不會有任何影響／毫無作用」。
generatedExample: It won't make the smallest difference whether we start now or later.

explanationZhTw: 特地費心去做某事；常帶有「明明可以不做，卻還是願意多花工夫」的語氣。
generatedExample: She went to the trouble of calling every guest herself.

explanationZhTw: 盤問；訊問，通常指正式、強勢、反覆地問問題，像警察或審問者的語氣。
generatedExample: The detective spent hours interrogating the witness.

explanationZhTw: 喻指結構中的缺陷；這裡是把溫斯頓當成體制裡必須清除的「異物」。
generatedExample: In their eyes, one mistake made her a flaw in the pattern.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「徹底消滅、清除」。
generatedExample: The virus was wiped out after months of vaccination.

explanationZhTw: 指「迫害者」，也就是對他人施加殘酷壓迫的人。
generatedExample: The protesters accused the regime of acting like persecutors.

explanationZhTw: 指極度卑微、完全屈服的狀態；語氣很強烈。
generatedExample: He offered abject submission to avoid further punishment.

explanationZhTw: 表示「投降、屈服」；這裡指把意志交出去。
generatedExample: The team refused to surrender despite the pressure.

explanationZhTw: 指「異端、持不同信仰或思想者」，帶有宗教與政治迫害色彩。
generatedExample: In medieval times, a heretic could be punished severely.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 capture 不是物理上的「抓住」，而是控制並改造一個人的思想。
generatedExample: The campaign was designed to capture the public's inner mind.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「全心全意、徹底地」。
generatedExample: She supported the cause heart and soul.

explanationZhTw: 表示「錯誤的、不正確的」；比一般的 wrong 更正式。
generatedExample: The report contained an erroneous assumption.

explanationZhTw: 指「偏離、偏差」；這裡是偏離規定思想的任何差異。
generatedExample: Any deviation from the plan must be approved first.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指中世紀火刑用的木樁，也就是被綁上去處死的地方。
generatedExample: The prisoner was sentenced to burn at the stake in the story.

explanationZhTw: 指「大清洗、政治整肅」；在蘇聯歷史語境中特別強烈。
generatedExample: The book describes the fear caused by the purges.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，這裡是比喻「把反抗想法壓在心裡、藏起來」。
generatedExample: She kept her anger locked up all day.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，這裡是殘酷的委婉說法，意思是「把腦袋打爆／殺掉」。
generatedExample: The old lamp blew out during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「公開反對、抵抗」；不是字面上的「站出來對抗」。
generatedExample: Very few people stood out against the decision.

explanationZhTw: 比喻「被徹底洗淨」；在此指被改造成完全服從、彷彿無罪。
generatedExample: He wanted to be washed clean of his past mistakes.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「擊垮、使屈服」，可指身體或精神被壓垮。
generatedExample: The interrogation finally broke them down.

explanationZhTw: 表示「嗚咽、嗚嗚哭著」，帶有軟弱、受苦的感覺。
generatedExample: The child was whimpering after the fall.

explanationZhTw: 表示「卑躬屈膝、諂媚地低聲下氣」；語氣很負面。
generatedExample: He kept grovelling for forgiveness after the mistake.

explanationZhTw: 指「懺悔、悔罪」；帶有真心認錯的宗教色彩。
generatedExample: She spoke with real penitence about what she had done.

explanationZhTw: 比喻「只剩空殼的人」；表示人的意志、人格已被完全摧毀。
generatedExample: After years of abuse, they were shells of men.

explanationZhTw: 這裡帶反諷色彩，表面是「令人感動」，實際上在冷酷地描述洗腦後的愛。
generatedExample: Her story was touching, but also deeply unsettling.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「愛做夢的」，而是指聲音聽起來恍惚、飄忽，像人有點出神。
generatedExample: Her dreamy voice made the room feel strangely quiet.

explanationZhTw: 指強烈的亢奮、激昂狀態，語氣比一般的「興奮」更強。
generatedExample: After the speech, the crowd was full of exaltation.

explanationZhTw: 指「偽君子」或虛偽的人，表裡不一。
generatedExample: She called him a hypocrite because his actions did not match his words.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「壓得他很難受、很困擾」，不是政治上的「壓迫」。
generatedExample: The silence oppressed him more than the noise had.

explanationZhTw: 表示「來來回回、前後往返」的動作。
generatedExample: The boat rocked to and fro in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「救自己」，而是指設法自保、逃過對方的控制或懲罰。
generatedExample: You can't save yourself by lying about what happened.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「走上歧途、偏離正道」。
generatedExample: He went astray after leaving school and needed help getting back on track.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「被放過、免於懲罰」的意思，不是一般的「節省」。
generatedExample: She was spared because she told the truth immediately.

explanationZhTw: 表示「一直活到…結束」或「把剩餘的日子過完」。
generatedExample: He moved to the coast to live out his retirement in peace.

explanationZhTw: 正式說法，指一個人本來自然會活到的壽命終點。
generatedExample: The policy was designed to protect the data for the natural term of your life.

explanationZhTw: 表示「事先、提前」。
generatedExample: Please tell me in advance if you need a ride.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻性用法，指把人徹底打垮、壓到失去反抗力。
generatedExample: The constant pressure can crush you down if you never take a break.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「無法回頭、無法恢復原狀」。
generatedExample: After that decision, there was no coming back.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「恢復正常或復原」，不只是身體痊癒，也包括心理與人格上的修復。
generatedExample: It took her months to recover after the shock.

explanationZhTw: 表示「有能力做…／能夠…」。
generatedExample: After months of training, he was capable of leading the team.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指物體中空，而是形容人內在空洞、失去情感與自我。
generatedExample: She felt hollow after the argument ended.

explanationZhTw: 比喻「把你榨乾」，讓你失去所有力量、情感或資源。
generatedExample: The endless overtime squeezed him empty.

explanationZhTw: 停頓；暫時停止動作或說話。這裡表示他先停了一下。
generatedExample: She paused before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: 向某人做手勢示意；這裡不是「簽名」，而是用手勢無聲地傳達意思。
generatedExample: He signed to me to keep quiet.

explanationZhTw: 知道的；察覺到的。這裡表示溫斯頓感覺到後方正在發生的事。
generatedExample: I was aware that someone was watching me.

explanationZhTw: 器械；裝置。通常指為某種用途設計的一套設備，這裡是比較正式或技術性的用法。
generatedExample: The laboratory has expensive apparatus for testing samples.

explanationZhTw: 在……旁邊。表示位置靠近、緊挨著某物。
generatedExample: She sat beside her friend on the bus.

explanationZhTw: 和……同一高度；幾乎平齊。這裡是在描述臉部高度接近相同。
generatedExample: When he stood on the step, his eyes were on a level with mine.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不是「真的在溫斯頓頭上說話」，而是「不把溫斯頓當作對話對象，直接對另一個人說話」；屬於依語境理解的用法。
generatedExample: She answered over my head to the waiter, as if I weren't there.

explanationZhTw: 「稍微潮濕的」；這裡用來描述墊子的觸感，表示有一點濕潤，不是完全乾的。
generatedExample: The towel was slightly moist after being left in the bathroom.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「緊緊夾住、牢牢壓住」的意思，帶有強硬、無法輕易脫開的感覺。
generatedExample: The metal bands clamped themselves around the box.

explanationZhTw: 「畏縮、退縮」；通常指因恐懼、痛苦或壓力而本能地縮了一下。
generatedExample: She quailed when she heard the loud bang.

explanationZhTw: 「令人安心地」；表示動作的目的或效果是讓對方感到比較不緊張。
generatedExample: He smiled reassuringly before starting the test.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `hurt` 是常見的 A2 單字，意思是「疼、痛」；整句是在安撫對方，表示「這次不會痛」或「不會造成疼痛」。
generatedExample: Don't worry, the shot will not hurt much.

explanationZhTw: `fixed on` 在這裡不是「固定住」的字面意思，而是表示「盯著、注視著」；整句是在要求對方一直看著自己。
generatedExample: Keep your eyes fixed on the road when you drive.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，意為「毀滅性的、極嚴重的」。這裡用來強調爆炸帶來的強烈衝擊，不只是普通的「很糟」。
generatedExample: The storm caused devastating damage to the harbor.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「失明的」本意，而是指「強烈刺眼的」。用來形容光線亮到讓人一時看不清。
generatedExample: A blinding light came from the stage.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「被打得趴倒、癱倒在地，幾乎動不了」。不是日常最常見的用法，屬於較正式或文學性的表達。
generatedExample: The heat left him prostrated on the sofa.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是口語中常見的「很棒」，而是「非常猛烈的、驚人的」。用來加強那一擊的力量感。
generatedExample: She heard a terrific crash outside the house.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，字面是「把他壓平」，此處表示被猛烈的力量打倒在地，變成平躺或癱倒的狀態。
generatedExample: The impact flattened him out on the floor.

explanationZhTw: 表示「重新恢復焦點、重新看清楚」。這裡是眼睛從模糊狀態恢復正常視力的意思。
generatedExample: After a few seconds, her eyes regained their focus.

explanationZhTw: 比喻用法，指腦中或記憶裡「一塊空白、缺失的部分」。不是實際的空地，而是心理或認知上的空洞感。
generatedExample: There was a patch of emptiness in my memory after the accident.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `last` 不是名詞「最後」；它是動詞，意思是「持續、撐得住、維持下去」。整句是在說「它不會維持太久」。
generatedExample: The phone battery will not last until evening.

explanationZhTw: `citizen` 指「公民；國民」。這裡是說他屬於大洋國這個政體的人。
generatedExample: She became a citizen of Canada last year.

explanationZhTw: `be aware` 表示「知道、察覺到」。這裡是說他根本沒有意識到有戰爭存在。
generatedExample: I was not aware that the meeting had been moved.

explanationZhTw: 「at war with」表示「和……處於戰爭狀態／交戰中」。這裡是字面用法，不是比喻。
generatedExample: The two countries have been at war with each other for years.

explanationZhTw: 「be at war with」表示「與……交戰、處於戰爭狀態」。這裡不是單純說『有戰爭』，而是固定搭配，強調兩方正在打仗。
generatedExample: The two countries have been at war with each other for years.

explanationZhTw: 「without a break」是慣用說法，意思是「不間斷地、一直持續著」。這裡用來強調戰爭從未停止。
generatedExample: She worked for six hours without a break.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「傳奇人物」，而是「捏造出來的故事／說法」。在這段話中，這個 legend 指的是虛構內容。
generatedExample: They spread a legend to hide what really happened.

explanationZhTw: 表示「被判死刑」；condemned 是正式、嚴肅的語氣，整個片語常見於法律或歷史敘述。
generatedExample: The spy was condemned to death after the trial.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「背叛、叛國行為」，比一般的 betrayal 更正式，也更嚴重。
generatedExample: His treachery shocked everyone in the city.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「假裝、裝作」；表示故意表現成另一種樣子。
generatedExample: She pretended to be asleep.

explanationZhTw: 表示「證明他們無罪」；prove 在這裡是用證據證實某件事。
generatedExample: The new evidence proved her innocent.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「存在」；這裡強調那張紙根本從未出現過。
generatedExample: The old bridge still existed in the painting.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「發明、編造」；這裡是「捏造出來」的意思，不是發明實體物品。
generatedExample: He invented a story to explain the missing money.

explanationZhTw: 表示「逐漸開始相信」；grow to 表示經過一段時間後慢慢形成某種看法。
generatedExample: At first I doubted him, but I grew to believe his version.

explanationZhTw: `held up` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「舉起、抬起」。不是「阻礙」或「搶劫」那種常見延伸義。
generatedExample: She held up her hand to ask a question.

explanationZhTw: 「held up」這裡是「舉起、抬起」的意思，指把手指展示出來。
generatedExample: She held up three fingers to show the number.

explanationZhTw: 「concealed」表示「隱藏的、遮住的」，這裡是說拇指沒有被看見。
generatedExample: He concealed the key under a book.

explanationZhTw: 「fingers」是「手指」。這裡是非常字面、基礎的身體部位用法。
generatedExample: She counted the fingers on her left hand.

explanationZhTw: 「fingers」是「手指」。這裡是非常字面、基礎的身體部位用法。
generatedExample: She counted the fingers on her left hand.

explanationZhTw: 「yes」是最基本的肯定回應，表示同意、答應或確認；在這裡就是單純回覆對方。
generatedExample: "Yes," she said when I asked if she could help.

explanationZhTw: 指「轉瞬間、極短的一瞬」。這裡強調時間非常短，不是字面上「漂過的瞬間」。
generatedExample: I only caught a fleeting instant of the fireworks before the clouds covered them.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻用法，意思是「腦海中的景象／意識中的畫面」，不是實際的風景。
generatedExample: The scenery of her mind kept shifting as she tried to remember the accident.

explanationZhTw: 指「困惑、茫然」，常用來表示因不明白情況而產生的迷惑感。
generatedExample: His face showed bewilderment when the instructions changed at the last minute.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「擠過來」，而是表示情緒或記憶「一股腦兒湧回來」。
generatedExample: When I heard that song, memories came crowding back again.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「明亮而強烈的確信感」，是文學性的說法，不是日常直譯的「發光的確定」。
generatedExample: For a brief moment, she felt a luminous certainty that she had chosen the right path.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「填滿、補上」的意思，也可理解成把空白感補起來。
generatedExample: The new information filled up the gaps in our understanding.

explanationZhTw: 指「一塊空白、空虛感」，這裡不是實體的洞，而是心理上的缺口。
generatedExample: After the argument, he felt a patch of emptiness he could not explain.

explanationZhTw: 指「實際上、就某種意義而言」，常用來補充說明真實狀態。
generatedExample: She was in effect the team leader, even though her title was assistant manager.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `see` 不是指「看見」，而是口語中的「理解、明白」。整句是在說「你現在明白了吧」。
generatedExample: You see now why we needed more time.

explanationZhTw: `at any rate` 是慣用語，意思是「無論如何；至少；不管怎樣」。這裡用來表示前一句的重點是：至少這件事是可能的。
generatedExample: We may be late, but at any rate we will arrive today.

explanationZhTw: `possible` 是常見詞，意思是「可能的、可行的」。在這裡強調某件事並非做不到，只是先證明它可行。
generatedExample: It is possible to finish the work by Friday.

explanationZhTw: 「air」在這裡不是「空氣」，而是指「神情、態度、樣子」。整個片語表示一種滿意、得意的神情。
generatedExample: She walked into the room with a confident air.

explanationZhTw: 「ampoule」是醫療用語，指密封的小玻璃藥劑瓶，使用時通常要折開瓶頸。
generatedExample: The nurse opened the ampoule carefully before preparing the injection.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是醫療操作語境，指把注射器的活塞往後拉，以便吸取藥液。這種用法比日常英文更專門。
generatedExample: He drew back the plunger of the syringe to fill it with liquid.

explanationZhTw: 「resettled」在這裡不是「重新安置」的字面意思，而是指把眼鏡往上推正、扶正。
generatedExample: She resettled her glasses on her nose and kept reading.

explanationZhTw: diary 指「日記」，這裡是字面義，屬於常見基礎詞彙。
generatedExample: I wrote a short note in my diary before bed.

explanationZhTw: enemy 指「敵人、對手」，這裡用來和 friend 對比，表示完全相反的關係。
generatedExample: In the game, red is the enemy team.

explanationZhTw: appeal to me 這裡不是「上訴」，而是「對我有吸引力、合我胃口；讓我感興趣」。
generatedExample: That simple design really appeals to me.

explanationZhTw: insane 在這裡是「瘋狂的、精神失常的」，語氣很重，也帶有貶義。
generatedExample: He made an insane decision to drive through the storm.

explanationZhTw: bring ... to an end 是慣用說法，意思是「結束、收尾」。
generatedExample: Let's bring the meeting to an end before lunch.

explanationZhTw: `question` 在這裡是「問題；要問的話」的意思，屬於基礎常用詞。
generatedExample: She asked one question after another.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `like` 不是「喜歡」，而是表示「我想要的、我選的」；整句是在確認自己可以自由挑任何問題。
generatedExample: You can choose any color you like.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `upon` 不是字面上的「在……上面」，而是較書面或文學的用法，等於 `on`，表示「注視著、落在……上」。
generatedExample: All eyes were upon her when she stepped on stage.

explanationZhTw: `dial` 指儀表面、刻度盤或轉盤，這裡是機器上的錶盤。
generatedExample: Turn the dial until the number reaches ten.

explanationZhTw: `switched off` 是片語動詞，表示「關掉、切斷開關」。這裡指它已經被關閉。
generatedExample: Please switch off the lights before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`what have you done with + 人/物` 是慣用問法，意思不是單純「你和 Julia 做了什麼」，而是「你把 Julia 怎麼了／Julia 現在在哪裡／你對 Julia 做了什麼」。這是一種帶有質問、關切甚至指控意味的說法。
generatedExample: What have you done with my keys? I left them on the table five minutes ago.

explanationZhTw: betrayed 是「背叛、出賣」；這裡指把對方交給敵對一方或不再忠於對方。
generatedExample: He felt betrayed when his best friend shared the secret.

explanationZhTw: come over to us 是「轉而支持我們、倒向我們這邊」；在政治或立場語境中很常見。
generatedExample: After the debate, several voters came over to our side.

explanationZhTw: promptly 是「迅速地、立刻地」；這裡強調動作發生得很快。
generatedExample: Please reply promptly so we can finish the plan.

explanationZhTw: rebelliousness 是「叛逆性、反抗性」；指不服從權威或規則的傾向。
generatedExample: Her rebelliousness made her question every rule at school.

explanationZhTw: deceit 是「欺騙、詭詐」；表示故意用不誠實的方式誤導別人。
generatedExample: The company was fined for deceit in its advertising.

explanationZhTw: folly 是「愚蠢的行為、蠢事」；比單純的 stupid 更正式，也常帶有批評意味。
generatedExample: It would be folly to ignore the warning signs.

explanationZhTw: dirty-mindedness 是「思想下流、好色的心態」；這裡指帶有性暗示或不端正的想法。
generatedExample: His dirty-mindedness made the joke sound worse than it was.

explanationZhTw: burned out of her 在這裡不是字面上的「燒掉」，而是比喻「被徹底消除、被強行磨掉」的意思。
generatedExample: Years of pressure seemed to burn all the confidence out of him.

explanationZhTw: textbook case 是成語式說法，指「標準範例、典型案例」；表示非常符合教科書上的定義。
generatedExample: Her recovery was a textbook case of how early treatment helps.

explanationZhTw: 「tortured」在這裡是動詞過去式，意思是「對某人施以酷刑、折磨」；在這句話中帶有強烈的驚訝與指控語氣，表示說話者在確認是否真的對她施虐。
generatedExample: Did they really torture the prisoner during the interrogation?

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用搭配，意思是「沒有回應這件事／故意不回答」。不是字面上的「把它留成未答」。
generatedExample: She left my email unanswered for two days.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「下一個問題」，而是說話者在對話中要對方「換題／別再問這題了」。
generatedExample: When I asked about his salary, he said, "Next question."

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》脈絡中，`Big Brother` 不是單純的「哥哥」，而是指極權政權的象徵人物或監控權力的代表。這裡是專有的上下文意義，不是一般日常用法。
generatedExample: People fear that Big Brother is always watching them.

explanationZhTw: 表示「存在、是真的」；這裡是最基本、常見的用法，指大洋國的人相信他確實存在。
generatedExample: People say the building exists, but no one has seen it from inside.

explanationZhTw: 在《一九八四》裡是專有名詞，指統治社會的政黨，不是一般的「派對」或泛指政黨。
generatedExample: The Party controls every part of daily life.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是專有名詞，指極權統治的象徵性領袖或監控形象，不是字面上的「哥哥」。
generatedExample: In the novel, Big Brother is always watching.

explanationZhTw: 表示「具體化、化身、典型代表」；這裡指大洋國的領袖形象就是政黨理念的具體呈現。
generatedExample: She was the embodiment of patience and calm.

explanationZhTw: 「exist」表示「存在、活著、真實地在世上」，這裡是在問他是否以和我同樣的方式存在。
generatedExample: Do ghosts really exist, or are they just stories?

explanationZhTw: 「exist」表示「存在、活著、真實地在世上」，這裡是在問他是否以和我同樣的方式存在。
generatedExample: Do ghosts really exist, or are they just stories?

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是單純指「不存在」的哲學意義，而是帶有政治壓制的語境：你不被承認、沒有身分、彷彿從制度上被抹除。
generatedExample: After the records were destroyed, it was as if he did not exist.

explanationZhTw: 「無助感」；指一種覺得自己無能為力、無法控制情況的感受。
generatedExample: A sense of helplessness came over her when the plan failed again.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不是「攻擊」身體，而是「強烈湧上／侵襲」他，形容情緒或念頭突然壓過來。
generatedExample: Doubt assailed him before the interview started.

explanationZhTw: 「雙關語」；利用一個字或片語的兩種意思來造成巧妙效果。
generatedExample: The slogan was a play on words that made people smile.

explanationZhTw: 「邏輯上的荒謬」；指表面上看似有道理，但其實在推理上自相矛盾、站不住腳。
generatedExample: The argument was a logical absurdity, so nobody accepted it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，指「萎縮、變得退縮」；形容他的思緒或精神因恐懼而變弱。
generatedExample: Her confidence shrivelled after the harsh criticism.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「無法反駁的」；不是單純「不能回答」，而是辯論上很難有回應空間。
generatedExample: She raised an unanswerable point during the discussion.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「拆毀建築」，而是「徹底擊敗、駁倒」某人，特別是用言語或論證。
generatedExample: The lawyer demolished the witness's story with one question.

explanationZhTw: exist 指「存在」；這裡是哲學式的用法，表示「有真實存在」而不只是被提到。
generatedExample: Some people act as if the problem does not exist.

explanationZhTw: conscious 是形容詞，意思是「意識到的；有知覺的」。這裡是「清楚意識到」。
generatedExample: She was conscious of every sound in the room.

explanationZhTw: identity 是名詞，指「身分；自我認同」。這裡是對自身是誰的認知。
generatedExample: He struggled to build a clear identity after moving abroad.

explanationZhTw: occupy 在這裡不是「占領」，而是「佔據；處於某位置」。屬於較正式、具體的空間用法。
generatedExample: The sofa occupies most of the small living room.

explanationZhTw: particular 這裡是形容詞，表示「特定的；某一個」。
generatedExample: She asked about a particular book on the shelf.

explanationZhTw: simultaneously 是副詞，意思是「同時地」。這裡用來強調兩件事不能在同一瞬間發生。
generatedExample: The two alarms rang simultaneously.

explanationZhTw: 「of no importance」表示「不重要、無關緊要」。這裡不是逐字理解成「沒有重要性」的抽象說法，而是自然地表達「這件事不重要」。
generatedExample: The meeting time is of no importance as long as we finish today.

explanationZhTw: 在《一九八四》中，Big Brother 不是字面上的「哥哥」，而是指極權政權的象徵人物或監控權力本身。
generatedExample: People in the novel fear that Big Brother is always watching them.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Of course」不是在字面上表示「當然」，而是用來加強否定，意思接近「那當然不是」或「那是不可能的」。
generatedExample: Of course I’ll help you finish the report.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，表示「下一個問題／別再問這個了」，語氣帶有不想繼續回答或要轉移話題的意思。
generatedExample: I’m not discussing that now. Next question.

explanationZhTw: 「Brotherhood」在這裡不是一般的「兄弟情誼」，而是指小說中的秘密反抗組織或地下團體。
generatedExample: People whispered that the Brotherhood still existed, even though no one could prove it.

explanationZhTw: 「set ... free」表示「釋放、讓某人自由」。這裡不是單純描述自由，而是帶有「解除控制、放走」的意思。
generatedExample: They promised to set the hostages free at dawn.

explanationZhTw: 「finish with someone」在這裡是「不再需要某人、對某人做完該做的事」；在上下文中暗示審訊或利用結束後才會放人。
generatedExample: The doctor said she would be in in a minute, after she had finished with the previous patient.

explanationZhTw: 「as long as」表示「只要、在……期間」。這裡強調這個狀態會持續到你活著的整個期間。
generatedExample: As long as you study every day, your English will improve.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「riddle」不是字面上的「謎語」，而是指「無法解開的謎團、始終想不通的問題」。
generatedExample: For years, the disappearance remained an unsolved riddle.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `had got to` 表示「不得不、一定要」；不是「已經得到」，而是表達強烈的義務或必要性。
generatedExample: I had got to finish the report before noon.

explanationZhTw: `utter` 在這裡是「說出、發出（話語）」；語氣比一般的 `say` 更正式，也更強調把話真正講出口。
generatedExample: She could not utter a word after the shock.

explanationZhTw: `trace` 在這裡是「一點點、少許」；`a trace of amusement` 就是「一絲笑意／些許覺得好笑」。
generatedExample: I detected a trace of sadness in his voice.

explanationZhTw: `wear` 這裡不是「穿戴」，而是「顯出、帶有」的意思；整個片語在描述眼鏡彷彿帶著反諷的神情。
generatedExample: Her smile seemed to wear a hint of regret.

explanationZhTw: `burst out` 是片語動詞，意思是「突然衝口而出、脫口而出」。
generatedExample: The answer burst out before I could stop myself.

explanationZhTw: 在《一九八四》中，Room 101 不是普通的「101 號房間」，而是象徵極度恐懼與精神折磨的特殊場所；這裡的意思要靠小說脈絡理解。
generatedExample: In the novel, Room 101 is the place every prisoner fears most.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `expression` 指「表情」或「臉色」，不是一般的「表達、表現」。
generatedExample: Her expression softened when she heard the news.

explanationZhTw: `drily` 在這裡是「冷淡地、沒什麼感情地」；不是指「乾燥地」。
generatedExample: He drily said that the plan would probably fail.

explanationZhTw: 「知道」；這是非常常見的基礎動詞，屬於 A2 級詞彙。
generatedExample: I know the answer now.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》脈絡中，這不是普通房間，而是指揭露一個人最深層恐懼、用來施以心理折磨的地方。
generatedExample: He was terrified when they told him he would be taken to Room 101.

explanationZhTw: 「每個人」；常見基礎代名詞，屬於 A2 級詞彙。
generatedExample: Everyone likes a short break.

explanationZhTw: 「知道」；這是非常常見的基礎動詞，屬於 A2 級詞彙。
generatedExample: I know the answer now.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》脈絡中，這不是普通房間，而是指揭露一個人最深層恐懼、用來施以心理折磨的地方。
generatedExample: He was terrified when they told him he would be taken to Room 101.

explanationZhTw: 「session」在這裡指一段正式進行中的活動或課程，像是治療、會議或訓練的一次進行，不是一般日常的「一段時間」而已。
generatedExample: The training session lasted for two hours.

explanationZhTw: 「at an end」表示「結束了、告一段落」，是常見片語，用來強調某件事已經完成或終止。
generatedExample: The argument was finally at an end.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「sank」不是字面上的「下沉」，而是比喻「迅速陷入」某種狀態，這裡指很快進入睡眠。
generatedExample: After the long hike, she sank into the sofa and fell asleep.
